work.title: Applied AI & DevEx Lead, Client Platforms

roles.title: Applied AI & DevEx Lead, Client Platforms
roles.description:
Leading adoption of AI-native development across Wealth Management Client Platforms, defining how `LLMs` are used across workflows, tooling, and the `SDLC`. Built internal tools and agentic workflows for code generation, testing, API development, and `CI/CD`, improving delivery speed by `4x`.

Created hands-on workshops and curriculum for practical `LLM` usage across engineering, and led `Nevernote` to OpenAI Hackathon finalist status using `Whisper`, `LangChain`, and `GPT`.

roles.title: Engineering Manager, Client Platforms
roles.description:
Led engineering strategy and delivery for `5` client-facing planning apps across `Morgan Stanley Online` and `E*TRADE`, managing `12 engineers` across `3 global squads`.

Influenced stakeholders to back a modern rebuild over a legacy-first approach, launching a unified platform that merged two experiences, reduced UX complexity, improved scalability, and saved `$2M+ annually`.

roles.title: Business Analyst -> UI Developer -> UI Development Lead
roles.description:
Progressed from Technical Business Analyst to Front-End Engineer to UI Development Lead. Led UI development for two zero-to-one financial planning applications in `Angular`, partnering closely with product and backend teams to ship and scale them.

Created technical and functional requirements, workflows, and dependency plans, while mentoring junior engineers and helping establish frontend best practices.

work.title: Founder
work.description: Founded mobile app helping food truck owners save time and money. Built and shipped end-to-end mobile and backend systems using `Flutter and Firebase`. Developed Garnish, our AI food truck advisor using `OpenAI` for menu pricing, permits, and operations guidance. Secured partner discounts with Square, Food Service Direct, and FLIP Insurance. Serving `150+` food trucks nationwide.

work.title: Co-Founder
work.description: Designed and built algorithmic trading systems using `Java`, `React`, and `AWS`, growing AUM to `$750K` in `18 months`. We exited in 2019.
